parentQuestion: The sympathetic division has short preganglionic fibers and long postganglionic fibers.

scaffolds.q: The autonomic nervous system uses TWO-neuron chains to reach target organs.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Both sympathetic and parasympathetic divisions use a preganglionic neuron (CNS to ganglion) and postganglionic neuron (ganglion to target). Two neurons, one synapse in between.

scaffolds.q: Sympathetic preganglionic neurons originate from the thoracolumbar spinal cord (T1-L2).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The sympathetic division is called 'thoracolumbar outflow' because all preganglionic cell bodies live in the lateral horn from T1 to L2.

scaffolds.q: Sympathetic ganglia are located CLOSE to the spinal cord.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Sympathetic ganglia (paravertebral chain and collateral ganglia) are near the spine. So preganglionic fibers don't have to travel far to reach them.

scaffolds.q: A SHORT preganglionic fiber means the ganglion is CLOSE to the CNS.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Sympathetic preganglionic neurons exit the spinal cord and quickly synapse in nearby ganglia. Short distance = short fiber.

scaffolds.q: After synapsing in the ganglion, postganglionic fibers travel to distant targets.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! From ganglia near the spine, sympathetic postganglionic fibers must travel long distances to reach the heart, blood vessels, lungs, etc.

scaffolds.q: LONG postganglionic fibers mean the target organs are FAR from the ganglia.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The ganglia are near the spine, but the targets (heart, lungs, skin) are far away. Long distance = long fiber.

scaffolds.q: Sympathetic architecture: Short preganglionic + long postganglionic.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Ganglia are near the spine (short pre-), but targets are far (long post-). This 'short-long' arrangement allows widespread, coordinated activation.

scaffolds.q: The sympathetic division has short preganglionic and long postganglionic fibers.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! This is the key anatomical feature. Contrast with parasympathetic (long pre-, short post-).

scaffolds.q: The statement is TRUE - sympathetic = short preganglionic, long postganglionic.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The answer is TRUE. Sympathetic ganglia are close to the spinal cord (short preganglionic) but far from targets (long postganglionic).
